name: Puente de Génave – return via Sierra de Segura
name_original: Puente de Génave – vuelta por Sierra de Segura
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Puente De Génave traverse the Sierra de Segura region in Jaén province, Andalusia. The landscape features a mix of olive groves, rolling hills, and mountainous areas, with the Guadalimar River flowing through the town. This terrain provides varied backdrops for road cyclists, characterized by wild scenery and a contrast between reddish earth and green vegetation. The area is part of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, indicating a preserved natural environment. Best road cycling routes around Puente De Génave The most popular road cycling route is Puente de Génave – return via Sierra de Segura, a 39.4 miles (63.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete, offering varied terrain through the Sierra de Segura. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ascent to the Castillo de Segura de la Sierra — circular by Orcera, a moderate 23.2 miles (37.4 km) path. This route features a significant ascent towards the historic Castillo de Segura de la Sierra. Local road cyclists also love the La Puerta de Segura, Torres de Albánchez, and Puente de Génave Loop, a 32.5 miles (52.4 km) trail leading through multiple towns and villages, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Road cycling around Puente De Génave is defined by mountainous areas, extensive olive groves, and the Guadalimar River valley. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Puente De Génave are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a small number of reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Puente De Génave's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Puente de Génave?
A: Road cycling routes around Puente de Génave offer a diverse landscape. You'll find a mix of extensive olive groves, rolling hills, and mountainous areas, particularly as you venture into the Sierra de Segura region. The terrain is characterized by wild scenery, with the reddish color of the earth contrasting beautifully with lush green vegetation. The Guadalimar River also flows through the town, providing picturesque riverside sections.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While the region is known for its varied terrain, including moderate and difficult options, there are routes that cater to different skill levels. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009283606/puente-de-genave-return-via-sierra-de-segura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Génave – return via Sierra de Segura&lt;/a&gt; route is rated moderate and offers a good introduction to the area's landscapes without being overly strenuous for experienced riders.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40302884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;C.F. El Campillo Shelter – Segura de la Sierra loop from Cementerio Municipal&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains, with over 2,300 meters of ascent over approximately 108 kilometers. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos de Segura – Hornos Viewpoint loop from Cementerio Municipal&lt;/a&gt;, featuring over 1,400 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Puente de Génave?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009268085/la-puerta-de-segura-torres-de-albanchez-and-puente-de-genave-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Puerta de Segura, Torres de Albánchez, and Puente de Génave Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through multiple towns and villages. Another excellent circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1277897445/ascent-to-the-castillo-de-segura-de-la-sierra-circular-by-orcera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Castillo de Segura de la Sierra — circular by Orcera&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a notable climb to a historic castle.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Puente de Génave?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle along the picturesque Guadalimar River, which features small waterfalls and old mills. The historic Puente Viejo (Old Bridge), dating back to Roman times, and the 19th-century Puente Nuevo (New Bridge) are notable landmarks. You'll also pass through extensive olive groves and enjoy panoramic views from various points within the Sierra de Segura. Keep an eye out for charming picnic areas and accessible drinking water sources along the way, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5713123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Drinking water source&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Puente de Génave?
A: The diverse landscapes of Puente de Génave can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, with comfortable conditions for tackling both the rolling hills and mountainous sections. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months. Winters are generally mild, making cycling possible, though some higher elevation routes might be cooler.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling options or areas suitable for children?
A: While many road cycling routes in the mountainous areas are more suited for experienced riders, the region offers a pleasant riverwalk along the Guadalimar River that can be enjoyed by families. Additionally, the broader Jaén province features the Via Verde del Aceite (Olive Oil Greenway), a 128-kilometer route on an old railway track, which is relatively flat and offers an easy cycling experience through olive groves, ideal for families looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: Where can I find places to rest or refuel along the road cycling routes?
A: Several charming picnic areas are found along the routes, offering tables and shade, perfect for a break. You'll also find accessible &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5713123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;drinking water sources&lt;/a&gt; in some areas, which are crucial for longer rides. The towns and villages you pass through, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Génave Village&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Puerta de Segura&lt;/a&gt;, will have local cafes and shops where you can refuel and experience local hospitality.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Puente de Génave?
A: The road cycling routes in Puente de Génave are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from olive groves to mountainous sections, and the wild, scenic backdrops. The combination of challenging climbs and picturesque river valleys makes for a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling tours starting from Puente de Génave?
A: Yes, Puente de Génave serves as an excellent starting point for longer road cycling adventures into the Sierra de Segura. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40302884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;C.F. El Campillo Shelter – Segura de la Sierra loop from Cementerio Municipal&lt;/a&gt;, at approximately 108 kilometers, provide a substantial challenge and allow you to explore a significant portion of the natural park and its surrounding villages.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of road cycling in Puente de Génave?
A: Road cycling around Puente de Génave offers a range of difficulty levels, primarily moderate to difficult. The terrain includes significant elevation changes as you navigate through mountainous areas and rolling hills. While there are options for less strenuous rides, many routes involve considerable climbing, making it a rewarding destination for cyclists looking for a challenge.

Q: Can I find parking easily for road cycling routes in Puente de Génave?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, Puente de Génave is a town with local amenities. You can typically find parking within the town or near the starting points of various routes. It's advisable to check local signage for any parking restrictions or designated areas, especially in the town center.
tips: It is a beautiful municipality on the banks of the Guadalimar river. It is located at the foot of the Sierra del Segura and is a strategic point for adventurers who want to explore it, both on foot and by bicycle.

Small picturesque town located at the entrance of the Sierra de Segura mountains. The town sits on the Guadalquivir river. It dates back to the 1st century BC, with the construction of a bridge over the Guadalquivir river, part of an important Roman road that existed in this valley and an important crossroads in trade between Levante and Andalusia.
